rationale: Both outputs correctly separate pre-deadline feasibility from the later optimality certificate and reject unsupported real-world benefit claims. A is the stronger standard-profile explanation because it is compact, well organized, and precise that the certificate supplies retrospective model-based assurance without improving the irreversible dispatch. B offers a particularly useful checklist of missing evidence, but it repeats the same distinctions across its table, managerial implication, and claim-boundary sections. Its visible “Eligibility” layer is not needed for this deliverable, and its statement that the solver spent a further 25 minutes certifying the solution is imprecise because the incumbent was found sometime before, not necessarily at, minute 10. B also frames assurance versus latency as a trade-off even though post-dispatch computation need not delay the already-executed decision. These are minor rather than material defects, but they make A more faithful and economical overall.
